english: pyin-nas (phyin nas) having proceeded about to the middle of the rope Dzl.; skyé-boi (skye bo'i) dbús-su (dbus su) in the midst of the people Tar.; taṅ (thang) ynyis-kyi (gnyis kyi) dbus-rí (dbus ri) the hill (mountain) in the middle between the two plains Glr.; dbús-kyi (dbus kyi) ri-rgyál (ri rgyal) Sumeru standing in the centre (of the world) Mil.; seldom relative to time: bzúgs-pai (bzhugs pa'i) dbús-su (dbus su) whilst he was sitting Glr.; in metaphysics: dbus (dbus) daṅ (dang) mta (mtha') 'the medium and the extremes' Cs. Asiat. Researches XX, 577 — dbús-ma (dbus ma) the middle one (of three or more persons) Mil., (of inanimate things) Glr. — 2. in a specific sense: the central province of a country, a. of India, hence = Magadha, the holy land, land of Buddha Thgy. b. of Tibet, the province Ü; dbús-pa (dbus pa) an inhabitant of it; dbus-ytsáṅ (dbus gtsang) Ü and Tsaṅ.

solitary, lonely, e.g. a road Dzl.; solitude, loneliness, dbén-pa (dben pa) _di-na ('di na) in this solitude Dzl.; dbén-par (dben par) _gró-ba ('gro ba) or ynás-pa (gnas pa) frq.; dben (dben), id.: dbén-la (dben la) dga (dga') Ma.; dben-(pai) (dben pa'i) ynas (gnas), sa (sa) solitary place, esp. hermitage; dbén-ynas (dben gnas) čén-po (chen po) brgyád-kyi (brgyad kyi) sa (sa) earth from the eight great hermitages, sacred places of pilgrimage in India Glr.; like bstoṅs-pa (bstongs pa) the word is construed with the instrum. case: mas (mas) dbén-pa (dben pa), solitary as to a mother, i.e. motherless; snyíṅ-pos (snying pos) dbén-pa (dben pa) = snyíṅ-po (snying po) méd-pa (med pa) Tar.
1. n. of a lunar mansion, v. rgyu-skár (rgyu skar), no. 11. — 2. the belly-side of fur.

(W. *'ón-po* (*'on po*), C. *'om-po* (*'om po*)) 1. B. resp. for tsá-bo (tsha bo) grandson; nephew; dbon-srás (dbon sras) id. Glr.; dbón-mo (dbon mo) fem.; mes-dbón (mes dbon) ancestor and grandchild Glr.; dbon-žáṅ (dbon zhang) Glr. 95 seems to denote son-in-law and brother-in-law, with which also Sch.'s Mongol transl. agrees, Geschichte d. Ost-Mong. p. 359 med. — 2. Lama-servant C. — 3. a certain sect of Lamas, clad in red, shorn, and married, = *sor-kyím-pa* (*sor khyim pa*), C., W. — 4. a Lama skilled in astrology, who for instance, when a person has died, performs those ceremonies, that serve to avert harm from the survivors W.
Cs. = rtól-ba (rtol ba), Lex. rdziṅ (rdzing) dból-ba (dbol ba).

, *yaṅ(s)* (*yang(s)*) 1. singing, song, tune, melody, glu-dbyáṅs (glu dbyangs) id.; luṅ-bstán-gyi (lung bstan gyi) dbyaṅs (dbyangs) prophetic song, psalm Mil.; dbyaṅs(-su) (dbyangs su) lén-pa (len pa), dbyaṅs (dbyangs) byéd-pa (byed pa) to sing Dzl.; stód(-pai) (stod pa'i) dbyaṅs (dbyangs) song of praise, hymn of thanksgiving, *jhé-pa* (*jhe pa*) C., *púl-če* (*pul che*) W.; yduṅ-dbyáṅs (gdung dbyangs) a song of aspiration Mil. — dbyaṅs-čan (dbyangs can) Glr. a deity, prob. = _jam-dbyaṅs-čan-ma ('jam dbyangs can ma) Saraswati, goddess of euphony. — dbyaṅs-snyán (dbyangs snyan) sweet singing. — dbyaṅs-pa (dbyangs pa) singer Cs. — *yaṅ-žu* (*yang zhu*) bow for a violin, fiddle-stick W. — 2. vowel, hence dbyaṅs-yig (dbyangs yig) 1. the (four) signs of the vowels, Gram. 2. Cs.: notes (of music) or any contrivance for marking the modulation of sounds; so perh. also Glr.

summer, in India: rainy season (cf. dus (dus)); also dbyár-ka (dbyar ka), dbyár-k'a (dbyar kha) Mil., W., dbyár-dus (dbyar dus), Cs. dbyár-mo (dbyar mo); dbyar-dgun-méd-par (dbyar dgun med par) summer and winter Mil.; dbyar (dbyar) B., dbyár-ka-la (dbyar ka la) col. in summer; dbyar-ynás (dbyar gnas) 1. summer-abode, Sch. 2. the solitary summer-fasting of the monks; dbyar-skyés (dbyar skyes) 'summer-born'; dbyar-rṅá (dbyar rnga) summer-drum, po. expression for thunder Cs. — dbyar-čár (dbyar char) summer-rain Cs. — dbyar-žwa (dbyar zhwa) summer-hat.
(Pur. *sbyár-pa* (*sbyar pa*), elsewh. *yár-pa* (*yar pa*)) poplar, various kinds of which tree are found in the vicinity of villages, cultivated or growing wild. (Wdṅ. also sbyár-pa (sbyar pa).)
(*yi* (*yi*), com. *'i* (*'i*)) 1. lynx, dbyí-mo (dbyi mo) the female of this animal, dbyi-p'rug (dbyi phrug) a young one; dbyi-tsáṅ (dbyi tshang) lair of it. — 2. in Ü: beer, = čaṅ (chang).
= dbyig-gu (dbyig gu) little stick, cf. dbyig-pa (dbyig pa).
prob. only fut. to _pýi-ba ('phyi ba), to wipe off, to blot out, to efface, Lex.: rí-mo (ri mo), a drawing. Sch. however notices also a perf. dbyis (dbyis).
medicinal herb, used against delirium Med.; Cs.: 'a plant
